title: Sundar Pichai biography (Wikipedia, formation facts)
used_for: Pichai formation: born 1972 Madurai; father electrical engineer at GEC, mother stenographer; IIT Kharagpur (metallurgy), Stanford MS, Wharton MBA; Applied Materials/McKinsey; joined Google 2004 (Toolbar/Chrome/Android); Google CEO 2015, Alphabet CEO Dec 2019

title: Ruth Porat biography (Wikipedia, formation facts)
used_for: Porat formation: born 1957 England; father Dan Porat physicist (Holocaust survivor, SLAC 26 yrs); Stanford BA, LSE MSc, Wharton MBA; Morgan Stanley 1987-2015 (CFO 2010); advised US Treasury 2008 crisis (Fannie/Freddie/AIG); Google/Alphabet CFO since 2015

title: Larry Page biography (Wikipedia + Academy of Achievement, formation facts)
used_for: Page formation: born 1973 East Lansing MI; father Carl Page CS professor at Michigan State, mother taught programming; Montessori schooling; Univ of Michigan BS computer engineering, Stanford CS PhD (unfinished); founded Google 1998; vocal-cord paralysis; stepped down as Alphabet CEO Dec 2019, retains voting control

title: Sergey Brin biography (Wikipedia, formation facts)
used_for: Brin formation: born 1973 Moscow; Jewish family emigrated from USSR 1979 (antisemitism); father Michael Brin math professor Univ of Maryland, mother Eugenia NASA Goddard researcher; Montessori; Univ of Maryland BS, Stanford CS PhD (unfinished); co-founded Google 1998; President of Alphabet until Dec 2019; returned to AI/Gemini work (WSJ, July 2023); retains voting control

title: Page and Brin control of Google voting power (Fortune, Dec 5 2019)
used_for: Control mechanics: Class B = 10 votes/share; Page + Brin together control 84.2% of Class B = 51.2% of all votes; Class B holders: Page 42.9%, Brin 41.3%, Schmidt 8.6%, Doerr 2.4%, Drummond 0.03%; founders kept control after leaving operations

title: Anat Ashkenazi appointed Alphabet CFO (The CFO)

title: Activist hedge fund TCI urges Alphabet to cut head count and costs (MediaPost / TCI letter to Pichai)
used_for: Governance / cost-discipline dark-arc context: Christopher Hohn (TCI, ~$6B Alphabet stake) Nov 15 2022 letter to Pichai — 'too many employees and the cost per employee is too high'; headcount ~187,000 (Q3'22), >2x since 2017; urged cost cuts + larger buybacks. Pre-dates the Jan 2023 layoffs

title: ChatGPT popularity leads Google to declare 'Code Red'; Pichai increases AI involvement (Benzinga)
used_for: Founder re-engagement / competitive-existential context (Dec 2022): after ChatGPT, Google reportedly declared a company-wide 'Code Red'; Pichai stepped up AI involvement; Page and Brin (stepped down 2019) reportedly summoned to meetings on the response.
